run_metadata.coverage_summary: Live discovery on 2026-06-27 (Sydney) via WebSearch. Tier 1 swept across all nine jurisdictions (Commonwealth/AusTender, NSW eTendering/buy.nsw, Buying for Victoria, QTenders QLD + supply.qld.gov.au migration, Tenders WA, Tenders SA, Tenders Tasmania, NT QTOL, ACT Tenders). New open opportunities captured on NSW eTendering/buy.nsw (HW1 Bulli Pass slope stabilisation closing 01-Jul; Youth Justice in-vehicle video recording closing 30-Jun; The Rivers Secondary College Lismore classroom upgrade and Sydney Opera House AV services both closing 06-Jul), plus a new local-government tender (Inner West Council Thirning Villa refurbishment via VendorPanel, closing 03-Jul) and a Sydney Water GBE opportunity (Quakers Hill WRRF Advanced Treatment Package C). Nine still-open opportunities from the 2026-06-26 pass were carried forward with refreshed status. Tier 2 (GBE/public sector) extended this run with newly recorded buyer channels: Transgrid (ibuy/SAP Ariba), Ausgrid (TenderLink e-tendering; BESS Delivery Panel), CSIRO (AusTender + supplier portal) and TAFE NSW (buy.nsw + SAP Business Network). Tier 3 (private sector) executed and mandatory: SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network plus major-buyer portals confirmed and newly recorded — Qantas (Coupa), Coles (SAP Ariba Network + RangeMe) and Woolworths (SAP Ariba) — alongside the previously-recorded BHP, Rio Tinto, Fortescue, Woodside and Santos channels; all corporate opportunities remain behind free supplier registration or invitation-only, so they are recorded as discovered_sources and no corporate tender was fabricated. CRITICAL LIMITATION (unchanged from prior run): every buyer detail page tried (tenders.gov.au, buy.nsw, sydneywater.com.au, innerwest.nsw.gov.au) returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool (bot/anti-scraping protection), so all opportunity detail is from buyer-originated search-result snippets only; no tender document could be opened this run.
gaps: All buyer-originated tender detail pages tried this run (tenders.gov.au, buy.nsw.gov.au, tenders.nsw.gov.au, sydneywater.com.au, innerwest.nsw.gov.au) returned HTTP 403 to WebFetch (bot/anti-scraping protection). Detail therefore comes from buyer-originated search snippets only and could not be confirmed against the notices' own documents; tender IDs, values, evaluation criteria and contacts remain unverified for every entry.

Conflicting closing date for the Moss Vale Road / Barrengarry Mountain slope-stabilisation tender: the prior run recorded 06-Jul-2026 while a fresh buy.nsw snippet this run showed 29-Jun-2026. Kept the prior value (06-Jul) pending confirmation; the notice could not be opened (403) to resolve the discrepancy.

WA, QLD, TAS, NT and ACT Tier 1 portals confirmed live this run but no current opportunity with a concrete closing date could be extracted from snippets (Tenders WA search truncates on too many records; QLD is mid-migration to VendorPanel via supply.qld.gov.au).

Tier 3 corporate opportunities (SAP Ariba Discovery/SBN; BHP, Rio Tinto, Fortescue, Woodside via Ariba; Santos and Qantas via Coupa; Coles and Woolworths via Ariba) are registration-gated or invitation-only and not publicly scrapable; no authenticated supplier session is available, so portals are recorded but no corporate tender entries were fabricated. Management-consultancy RFPs (Accenture/KPMG/Deloitte) remain out of scope (invitation-only).

EstimateOne / construction subcontractor work-packages and the Ausgrid TenderLink BESS Delivery Panel require a free trade/supplier account to view live package closing dates; platforms remain recorded as sources, individual packages not captured.

The Sydney Water Quakers Hill WRRF (Package C) opportunity had no closing date exposed in the snippet and the Sydney Water tenders page was not openable (403); recorded headline-only, pending a detail pass.
recommended_seed_additions: Transgrid — Suppliers (https://www.transgrid.com.au/about-us/suppliers/); NSW/national electricity transmission GBE; sourcing via SAP Ariba ("ibuy").

Ausgrid — Procurement and suppliers (https://www.ausgrid.com.au/About-Us/Procurement-and-suppliers); NSW distribution GBE; buyer-hosted e-tendering at portal.tenderlink.com/Ausgrid (BESS Delivery Panel).
CSIRO — Tenders & procurement (https://www.csiro.au/en/work-with-us/Working-with-CSIRO/Tenders-procurement); Commonwealth public research body; tenders >$400k via AusTender.
TAFE NSW — Supplying to TAFE NSW (https://www.tafensw.edu.au/partnerships/suppliers/supplying-to-tafe-nsw); opportunities on buy.nsw + SAP Business Network.
Qantas — Coupa Supplier Portal (https://www.qantas.com/au/en/about-us/our-company/our-departments/procurement/supplier-portal.html); corporate transport buyer.
Coles Group — Suppliers (https://www.colesgroup.com.au/suppliers/); corporate retail; GNFR via SAP Ariba Network, products via RangeMe.
Woolworths Group — Suppliers (https://www.woolworthsgroup.com.au/au/en/suppliers.html); corporate retail; non-trade sourcing via SAP Ariba.
Inner West Council — Current tenders (https://www.innerwest.nsw.gov.au/tenders-and-contracts/current-tenders); NSW local government on VendorPanel.

tenders.title: Air Transport and Remote Health Services for Regional and Rural NSW (Far West LHD)
tenders.buyer: NSW Ministry of Health / Far West Local Health District
tenders.description: NSW Health is seeking a provider (or providers) to deliver air transport and a bundle of remote health services across regional and rural NSW, anchored in the Far West Local Health District.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Provision of inter-hospital patient transfers and fixed-wing transport for clinicians, plus delivery of remote/rural health services including medical primary health care clinics, remote oral health services, and drug and alcohol information and counselling services across the Far West Local Health District and regional/rural NSW.
key_deliverables: Inter-hospital patient transfers across regional and rural NSW
Fixed-wing transport for clinicians to provide health services in rural and remote communities
Medical primary health care clinics in Far West LHD
Remote oral health services in Far West LHD
Drug and alcohol information and counselling services in Far West LHD
tenders.eligibility: Open (publicly advertised RFT via NSW eTendering).
tenders.delivery_location: Far West Local Health District, regional and rural NSW
tenders.detail_source: Scope reconstructed from GovMarket aggregator listing snippet (discovery signal only); buyer-originated NSW eTendering page could not be opened (HTTP 403).
tenders.reasoning: Carried forward from the 2026-06-26 prior pass; originally discovered via a GovMarket aggregator listing used only as a discovery signal and recorded against the buyer-originated source (NSW eTendering / NSW Ministry of Health). Aggregator is NOT treated as the source of truth.
tenders.notes: Carry-forward, still open (closing 16-Jul-2026). Service scope is snippet-derived via an aggregator and still needs confirmation on the buyer's own NSW eTendering notice; RFT number, value and contacts unverified.

tenders.title: Graphic and Digital Marketing Services to support industry engagements
tenders.buyer: Department of Defence — Capability Acquisition and Sustainment Group (Industry Engagement Division), DSRG
tenders.description: Defence's Industry Engagement Division (Capability Acquisition and Sustainment Group) is seeking graphic and digital marketing services plus event-management support for virtual events, to support delivery of international trade events and promote Australian defence industry.
tenders.deliverables_summary: Provision of graphic design and digital marketing collateral together with event-management support for virtual/online events, used to deliver international trade events and promote the Australian defence industry to international and domestic audiences.
key_deliverables: Graphic design and digital marketing materials
Support for delivery of international trade events
tenders.eligibility: Open to all suppliers (publicly advertised RFT).
tenders.detail_source: AusTender Current ATM View snippet for ATM 80064 (search result); full ATM page and documents returned HTTP 403.
tenders.reasoning: Carried forward from the 2026-06-26 prior pass; re-confirmed via AusTender keyword search as still open (closing 29-Jun-2026). Buyer-originated on the Commonwealth AusTender portal.
tenders.notes: Carry-forward, closing-soon. Scope, closing time and delivery timeframe (08/07/2026) taken from the AusTender search snippet; the ATM detail page and any attached documents could not be opened (HTTP 403), so values, evaluation criteria and contact remain unverified.

discovered_sources.notes: Detail pages returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool this run; data via search snippets only.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Canonical seed; the open-opportunities search on buy.nsw yielded several July-2026 Transport for NSW tenders this run.
discovered_sources.notes: Listing rows readable via search snippets; both buy.nsw and the legacy tenders.nsw.gov.au RSS/detail pages returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Canonical seed; queried this run (open + 'new this week' views).
discovered_sources.notes: Detail/list pages returned HTTP 403 to the fetch tool; current open VIC opportunities not extractable from snippets this run (VRMC2026 had already closed Dec-2025).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Canonical seed; central WA public-sector RFT portal (Main Roads WA, Water Corporation, regional commissions).
discovered_sources.notes: Confirmed live; no concrete current opportunity with a closing date extractable from snippets this run.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 WA GBE sweep; Water Corporation runs tendering via SAP Ariba and advertises on Tenders WA. 2026 Engineering Design Services Panel early tender advice issued.
discovered_sources.notes: Major capital works and panel opportunities; Ariba registration required to view/participate.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 sweep; Main Roads WA posts opportunities and Early Tender Advice via Tenders WA and a buyer-hosted e-tendering portal.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated state road agency. Portal is buyer-hosted (not a paid aggregator); registration required to access documents.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 1 SA sweep; SA Government opportunities portal complementing tenders.sa.gov.au.
discovered_sources.notes: Modern SA opportunities portal; pair with tenders.sa.gov.au for full SA coverage.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 1 QLD sweep; destination for the QTenders-to-VendorPanel migration (phased through 2025–2026).
discovered_sources.notes: Suppliers must register with VendorPanel to access documents and respond; check alongside the legacy QTenders directory (qtenders.epw.qld.gov.au).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 GBE sweep; Australia Post publishes tendering and supply opportunities on its corporate site.
discovered_sources.notes: Government Business Enterprise buyer; individual opportunities may require registration/contact.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 GBE sweep; NBN Co uses RFI/RFQ/RFP/RFT and contacts candidate suppliers directly.
discovered_sources.notes: Largely invitation/contact-based (supplierpolicies@nbnco.com.au); recorded as a source, opportunities not publicly listed.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 GBE sweep; Snowy Hydro runs renewable-energy and Snowy 2.0-related procurement; current tenders surfaced via the SERREE opportunities listing (discovery signal).
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated channel; confirm the live tenders page directly. SERREE used only as a discovery signal, not as the source of truth.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 mining sweep; Fortescue requires supplier pre-qualification and sources local suppliers via ICN/ePilbara for projects (Iron Bridge, Decarbonisation, Gibson Island).
discovered_sources.notes: Registration-gated; individual sourcing events require an authenticated account. No public RFx captured.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 energy sweep; Woodside uses a Supplier Engagement & SAP Ariba portal for registration, qualification and RFx.
discovered_sources.notes: Registration-gated (Ariba Network/Catalogues/We-buy). No public RFx captured.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 energy sweep; Santos uses a Coupa portal for supplier onboarding, safety prequalification and procurement participation.
discovered_sources.notes: Registration-gated via Coupa. No public RFx captured.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 VIC GBE sweep; Melbourne Water runs panels and RFTs (e.g. Field Civil Panel, engineering services, water pipe/valve supply).
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated VIC water GBE; confirm live tender listing directly (snippet evidence via aggregator listings used only as a discovery signal).

discovered_sources.source_name: SAP Ariba Discovery / SAP Business Network Discovery
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Mandatory Tier 3 channel; re-checked this run.
discovered_sources.notes: Public posting board for corporate RFx; full opportunity detail requires a free SAP Business Network supplier account. No publicly visible AU opportunity captured without login this run.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Known major buyer; re-checked this run.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 GBE search for electricity transmission networks; Transgrid runs all sourcing (RFI/RFQ/RFP/RFT) through SAP Ariba branded "ibuy".
discovered_sources.notes: Suppliers must register on SAP Ariba Network to participate; individual events are registration-gated, so the portal is recorded rather than individual opportunities.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 GBE search for distribution networks; Ausgrid uses portal.tenderlink.com/Ausgrid as its own e-tendering tool (buyer-hosted, not the aggregator product).
discovered_sources.notes: A BESS (Battery Energy Storage System) Delivery Panel opportunity was referenced; package detail is registration-gated. Source = the buyer’s own Ausgrid TenderLink portal, not a paid aggregator.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 public-research-body search; CSIRO publishes procurements over $400k via AusTender and runs a supplier portal.
discovered_sources.notes: Tenders above $400k go through AusTender; the SAP Ariba-hosted CSIRO Supplier Portal was flagged for discontinuance. Check AusTender for live CSIRO ATMs.

discovered_sources.source_name: TAFE NSW — Supplying to TAFE NSW
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 TAFE/education search; TAFE NSW lists open opportunities on the buy.nsw opportunities hub and invites sourcing events via SAP Business Network.
discovered_sources.notes: Open opportunities surface on buy.nsw; some sourcing events are issued via SAP Business Network (registration-gated).

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 transport/logistics search; Qantas runs supplier onboarding and sourcing through the Coupa Supplier Portal (CSP), free to suppliers.
discovered_sources.notes: Opportunities are issued to suppliers via Coupa (registration-gated/invitation); no publicly-advertised RFx visible without login. Portal recorded, no tenders fabricated.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 retail search; Coles transacts with goods-not-for-resale suppliers via the Ariba Network (free standard account); product sourcing via RangeMe.
discovered_sources.notes: GNFR tenders run through Ariba Network; product suppliers via RangeMe. Registration-gated; recorded as a portal, no tenders fabricated.

discovered_sources.source_name: Woolworths Group — Suppliers (SAP Ariba / Partner Hub)
discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 3 retail search; Woolworths uses SAP Ariba ("guided buying"/iBuy) for non-trade goods and services procurement across its store network.
discovered_sources.notes: Non-trade sourcing via SAP Ariba; Partner Hub for trade suppliers. Registration-gated; recorded as a portal, no tenders fabricated.

discovered_sources.how_discovered: Tier 2 local-government search via VendorPanel public tenders; council publishes current tenders on its own VendorPanel space.
discovered_sources.notes: Buyer-originated council channel on VendorPanel; one live tender captured this run (Thirning Villa refurbishment).
